economic_notes: Band has wholly owned limited partnerships: Antares Construction LLP, Antares Project Services LLP, and a business interest in Lightfood Gas Station Limited Partnership. Also holds 50% ownership in Lilloet Salish Enterprises Ltd and interests in BC First Nations Gaming Revenue Sharing LP and GP Inc. Band provided loan guarantees to Bank of Montreal for these entities totalling over $5.5M.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED because financial information for related government business enterprises and partnerships was not available and not accounted for in the consolidated financial statements. GBEs excluded include Antares Construction LLP, Antares Project Services LLP, Lightfood Gas Station LP, and Lilloet Salish Enterprises Ltd (50% owned). Annual surplus on income statement is $317,394 but this reflects a deferred revenue adjustment of -$1,106,566 from long-term debt proceeds; annual surplus before that adjustment was $1,423,960. Segmented total expenses of $2,658,289 differ from statement of operations total of $3,854,050 due to allocation methodology differences in segment reporting. expenses_housing combines Band Housing ($185,911) and CMHC Housing ($49,581) = $235,492 plus Capital Fund ($185,986) for infrastructure/capital = shown separately. total_government_transfers calculated as ISC $2,575,148 + FNHA $629,122 + FNESC $34,274 + CMHC $28,276 + Province of BC $193,953 + ASETS $41818 + New Relationship Trust $40000 = $3,542,591 (approximate; St'at'imc Trust and BC Hydro treated as other transfers).
warnings: Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
